name: com.bovinelabs.timeline.physics
description: Physics-oriented Timeline tracks for velocity control and stateful trigger-driven prefab instantiation in DOTS projects.

samples.description: Physics timeline sample content for validating velocity and stateful trigger tracks.

Gallery of all 12 DOTS physics timeline tracks (Force, Velocity, Drag, Gravity/Kinematic/Filter/VelocityClamp/Ricochet overrides, Linear/Angular PID, Teleport, Stateful Trigger), each driving a Dynamic body on a static ground with primitive lanes and TextMeshPro labels.

Raw com.unity.physics.custom playground (NO timeline): an editor builder that fills the active scene's SubScene with labelled stations for every shape, all three motion types, friction/restitution materials, collision-response modes, and joints (ball-and-socket pendulum, edge-hinged door, hanging distance chain). Run Tools > BovineLabs > Samples > Build Physics Custom Showcase.
Exhaustive 13-column grid covering all 12 DOTS physics timeline tracks (Force, Velocity, Drag, Gravity/Kinematic/Filter/Clamp overrides, Linear/Angular PID, Teleport, Ricochet, Stateful Trigger).
Swept-collision trigger track: Instantiate / Condition / Force / Query clips on swept shapes for fast tunnelling weapons.
Physics sword swing driven by a PhysicsAngularPID motor applying torque to a dynamic pivot body.
Physics-based spline path following using PID velocity control along an authored spline.
Directional knockback via a custom PendingForce system: a body ringed by eight trigger zones, launched away from contact.
8-direction knockback from a single PhysicsTriggerQuery in DirectionSector mode, routing per-sector force reactions.
Data-driven directional knockback: trigger ring -> ConditionEvent -> Reaction -> ActionTimeline force, no custom system.
Intent-vs-External motion channel comparison: an attacker's knockback trigger either gets eaten by or ignores the victim's own drag clip, depending on the channel.
